task_completion.description: Read the patient's name from the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor window is open displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/task_patient.txt', revealing the target patient's name.

task_completion.description: Log into the LibreHealth EHR system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the LibreHealth EHR interface in a logged-in state (e.g., main dashboard or calendar view).

task_completion.description: Search for and select the target patient in the EHR.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreHealth EHR patient search screen or patient list displaying the exact patient name retrieved from the text file.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the patient's Demographics and Choices section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The selected patient's record is open, specifically showing the 'Demographics' window and the 'Choices' tab or section.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Administrator' as the Primary Care Provider and save.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Provider' or 'Primary Care Provider' dropdown field in the Demographics > Choices section is set to 'Administrator', and there is visual confirmation that the record has been saved or updated.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the LibreHealth EHR via the web browser GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed within a web browser navigating the LibreHealth EHR interface, not via curl requests, API scripts, or direct database SQL queries in the terminal.

integrity.description: The patient modified in the EHR matches the patient from the text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The name of the patient whose record is being edited in the browser exactly matches the name shown in the '/home/ga/Desktop/task_patient.txt' file.

integrity.description: The agent did not spoof the completion by editing local HTML or text files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the correct local URL for LibreHealth EHR, and the interface elements are genuine LibreHealth EHR components.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target patient's name is dynamically generated and stored in '/home/ga/Desktop/task_patient.txt'. The agent must log in using username 'admin'. The provider to be assigned is 'Administrator', which corresponds to the admin user in the database. The assignment must be done within the LibreHealth EHR 'Demographics' > 'Choices' section.
